title: A holiday that didn't go as planned
summary: Tell a light, short story about a trip that went wrong but ended well.
topic: A holiday that didn't go as planned
prompt: Write 150–250 words about a holiday or short trip that did not go as you planned. Keep the tone light. Use past simple and 'when / while' to connect events.
hintStarters: Last year, we planned a quiet weekend in…
In the end, we laughed because…
sampleText: Last August, my sister and I planned a quiet weekend trip to a small island near Athens. The plan was simple: take the morning ferry, find our small hotel, swim, eat fish, sleep. Everything went wrong from the first hour. We missed the ferry by two minutes because the taxi driver took the wrong street. While we were waiting for the next ferry, the sky turned grey and a thunderstorm started. By the time we arrived on the island, we were completely soaked. The owner of the hotel forgot our reservation, so we had to take a smaller room near the kitchen. The air conditioner was broken, and we could hear pots and pans all night. The next morning, my sister opened her suitcase and discovered that her clothes were also wet. She did not have anything dry to wear. We walked into the little town, both of us in old t-shirts, and we bought two big white shirts from a small shop. The owner laughed and gave us a small bottle of olive oil for free. We sat on the beach in our giant white shirts and ate fresh bread and tomatoes. The sun came out for one hour. In the end, we laughed about it more than any of our perfect holidays. Sometimes plan B is better.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: quên đặt phòng của chúng tôi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous + past simple ('while')
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'While we were waiting for the next ferry, the sky turned grey.' — Past continuous mô tả hành động đang diễn ra, past simple chỉ sự kiện xảy ra ngắt vào.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'While we were waiting for the next ferry, the sky turned grey.' Past continuous shows the ongoing action; past simple shows the event that interrupted it.

sampleTranslationVi: Tháng Tám năm ngoái, chị em tôi lên kế hoạch một chuyến đi cuối tuần yên tĩnh đến một đảo nhỏ gần Athens. Kế hoạch đơn giản: bắt phà sáng, tìm khách sạn nhỏ, tắm biển, ăn cá, ngủ. Mọi thứ trật bánh ngay từ giờ đầu. Chúng tôi lỡ phà hai phút vì tài xế taxi đi nhầm đường. Trong khi chờ chuyến phà tiếp theo, trời chuyển xám và một cơn bão sấm sét nổi lên. Khi đến đảo, chúng tôi ướt sũng. Chủ khách sạn quên đặt phòng của chúng tôi, nên phải nhận một phòng nhỏ hơn cạnh bếp. Máy lạnh hỏng, và chúng tôi nghe tiếng xoong nồi suốt đêm. Sáng hôm sau, chị tôi mở vali và phát hiện quần áo cũng bị ướt. Chị không có gì khô để mặc. Chúng tôi đi vào phố nhỏ, cả hai mặc áo phông cũ, và mua hai chiếc áo trắng to ở một cửa tiệm. Bà chủ cười và tặng thêm một chai dầu ô liu nhỏ. Chúng tôi ngồi trên bãi biển trong những chiếc áo trắng rộng thùng thình, ăn bánh mì tươi và cà chua. Mặt trời ló ra một giờ. Cuối cùng, chúng tôi cười về chuyến đi đó nhiều hơn bất kỳ kỳ nghỉ hoàn hảo nào. Đôi khi phương án B lại hay hơn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses past simple?
options: We missed the ferry by two minutes.
We miss the ferry by two minutes.
We have missed the ferry by two minutes.
We are missing the ferry by two minutes.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'missed' for a finished event.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct 'while + past continuous' sentence.
options: While we waiting for the next ferry, the sky turned grey.
While we were waiting for the next ferry, the sky turned grey.
While we wait for the next ferry, the sky turns grey.
While we have waited for the next ferry, the sky has turned grey.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'While + past continuous, past simple' is the natural pattern.

options: The air conditioner was breaking.
The air conditioner was break.
The air conditioner was broken.
The air conditioner was breaked.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Broken' is the past participle used as an adjective.
mcQuestions.hint: Past participle used as adjective: broken.

options: The owner forgot our reservation, because we had to take a smaller room.
The owner forgot our reservation, but we had to take a smaller room.
The owner forgot our reservation, while we had to take a smaller room.
The owner forgot our reservation, so we had to take a smaller room.
My sister did not have anything dry to wear.
My sister did not had anything dry to wear.
My sister has not anything dry to wear.
My sister not have anything dry to wear.
Sometimes plan B is more better than plan A.
Sometimes plan B is better than plan A.
Sometimes plan B is the best than plan A.
Sometimes plan B is gooder than plan A.
Everything went badly from the first hour.
Everything go wrong from the first hour.
Everything went wrong from the first hour.
Everything gone wrong from the first hour.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Go wrong' is the fixed phrase; past simple = 'went'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses past simple of 'find' correctly?
options: She is discovering that her clothes were also wet.
She discover that her clothes were also wet.
She has discovering that her clothes were also wet.
She discovered that her clothes were also wet.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'discovered' matches the past timeline.

options: The sun came out for one hour.
The sun is coming out for one hour.
The sun came out since one hour.
The sun has come out for one hour ago.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'came out' + duration 'for one hour'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which closing line keeps a light, personal tone?
options: In conclusion, travel always has surprises.
Sometimes plan B is better.
Throughout history, holidays have tested patience.
It is important to note that flexibility is key.
mcQuestions.explanation: Short, personal sentence beats generic AI phrasing.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why are 'bad trips' often the best stories later? What does that say about how we remember our holidays?
